result:
All work complete. Plain-text summary follows.

---

UNIT D console/CLI — Round 7 (non-diff lemma-verdict family) — DONE. All 59 tests green (55 prior + 4 new); clean build, no warnings.

ACCESS COMPLIANCE: Read only PROTOCOL.md, console/SPEC.md, the workspace (BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log/cli-clean), .spthy example inputs, and my own captured outputs. Did NOT read anything under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, no *.hs, and not INTEGRATION_REPORT.md. All oracle runs went through probes/split_probe.sh under timeout, bounded/tiny theories only.

KEY FINDING — a FOURTH non-diff verdict phrase, previously unrenderable:
  `analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms)`
Reproduced first on csf23-subterms/YellowTest.spthy --prove (the known captured fact): YellowRed/YellowGreen both print `... (reducible operators in subterms) (4 steps)`.
Pinned properties (all from split-stream captures):
- UNIFORM across trace-kinds — identical for exists-trace and all-traces, NO falsified-style `- found trace`/`- no trace found` suffix (behaves like verified/incomplete). Fixed canned string; parenthetical reason stays plural regardless of count.
- Carries NO warning/advisory: the summary has no WARNING section and no "The analysis results might be wrong!" line; stderr is the plain 5 phases + `[Saturating Sources] Done`. So this proof-incompleteness state does NOT feed the §12b might-be-wrong advisory (that advisory tracks a wellformedness failure under --prove, confirmed unchanged).
- Proven-terminal: a DEFAULT (no --prove) run of the same theory shows all lemmas `analysis incomplete (1 steps)` — the phrase only appears when the proof actually runs and hits the UNFINISHABLE wall.
- COMPOSES with --diff prefixes and both kinds: YellowDiffTest --diff --prove renders `LHS :  <name> (<kind>): analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms) (3 steps)` for both exists-trace and all-traces, with RHS projections closing independently — same shared verdict path as whole-theory lemmas.

REST OF THE FAMILY — systematically confirmed to add NO new phrase:
- Bound exhaustion (--bound=2) → `analysis incomplete` (the bound cuts proofs short BEFORE the wall; DISTINCT verdict for the identical lemmas — directly answers the round-7 bound question).
- --stop-on-trace=SORRY → no change (reducible phrase for wall lemmas, verified/falsified otherwise).
- Induction ([use_induction], both kinds) → normal verified/falsified, no distinct phrase/error.
- [sources]/typing: --auto-sources renders the auto-generated lemma as a plain `AUTO_typing (all-traces): verified` line; unresolved partial deconstructions loop forever (timeout, empty stdout) — a resource condition, no summary line.
- Lemma errors / --prove non-match → abort with runtime stderr line or feed the WARNING count-line slot (unchanged from §14e/§12b).

IMPLEMENTATION:
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/src/framing.rs — added `LemmaResult::AnalysisCannotBeFinished` variant (+ per-variant doc comments) and its arm in `verdict_phrase` returning the canned string. Because Whole/Rhs/Lhs/Diff all route through `verdict_phrase`, side-prefixed and both-kind rendering picks it up with no per-side code.
- /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/tests/cli_tests.rs — 4 new byte-parity tests (split streams where applicable): `summary_reducible_operators_whole_theory_both_kinds`, `summary_bound_exhaustion_is_incomplete_not_unfinishable`, `diff_summary_projected_reducible_operators_both_sides_and_kinds`, `reducible_operators_line_bytes_are_exact`.
- New fixtures in cli-clean/tests/fixtures/: r7_yellow_prove.{out,err}.txt, r7_yellow_bound.{out,err}.txt, r7_yellowdiff_prove.out.txt (diff stderr left opaque per §14d).

DISCIPLINE ARTIFACTS:
- BEHAVIOR.md — new §15 (15a phrase + properties, 15b bound-distinct, 15c diff-composition, 15d other-states-null, 15e clean-crate model), each fact tagged with its r7_* capture. Report folded into BEHAVIOR.md (no REPORT.md exists; consistent with prior rounds).
- QUERIES.log — Round 7 section, 10 logged oracle interactions with provenance.
- Probe theory: probes/round7/induction.spthy (self-authored); captures under workspace/captures/r7_*.

Round-7 section appended to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/AUDIT.md`. Audit complete.

Delta audited (working tree over clean-room HEAD d1ada4b, `console/` only): one production-source change in `cli-clean/src/framing.rs` (new `LemmaResult::AnalysisCannotBeFinished` variant + `verdict_phrase` arm + doc comments), 4 new tests in `cli_tests.rs`, BEHAVIOR.md §15, QUERIES.log r7 entries, one self-authored probe (`probes/round7/induction.spthy`), and 10 captures / 5 fixtures.

Both-sides comparison against `lib/theory/src/Theory/Proof.hs` (`showProofStatus`/`showDiffProofStatus` 1104–1121), `ProofMethod.hs` (`Unfinishable`/`DiffUnfinishable`), and `Main/Mode/Batch.hs`. Key results:
- The one new verbatim overlap — `"analysis cannot be finished (reducible operators in subterms)"` — is an output-interface token that appears in captured tool stdout (`r7_yellow_prove`, `r7_yellow_sotsorry`, `r7_yellowdiff_prove`), logged QUERIES.log 15:05/15:09. Merger/interop, obtained by black-box observation, not lifted from Proof.hs. Filtered.
- Structure is reconstruction, not transcription: `verdict_phrase` matches on the clean's own 4-value `LemmaResult`, not upstream's dual `(quantifier, status)` functions; the two still-unprobed statuses (`analysis undetermined`, `proof has been invalidated`) remain absent; variant named `AnalysisCannotBeFinished`, not `UnfinishableProof`. No upstream identifier/comment lineage.
- Doc comments paraphrase (do not echo upstream's `"The proof cannot be finished (due to reducible operators in subterms)"` Haddock).
- Provenance clean: every §15 claim traces to a logged r7 probe + inspected capture; fixtures byte-identical to captures (`diff -q`); GPL example theories (YellowTest/YellowDiffTest/FreshOrderingTest/ParserTests) referenced by absolute `examples/` path only, NOT vendored (grep over `console/**/*.spthy` returns nothing). All 59 tests pass through the unchanged render path.

No copied protectable expression; no behavioral-redo instructions required.

VERDICT: pass
